mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization that supports equity valuations, but only during periods of stable inflation expectations.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between INDPRO growth and 20-day returns reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption that sustains consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 12-month windows when tested against 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payout capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over one month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with subsequent 20-day market returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production signal supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand that force immediate equity repricing downward within five trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
